task_completion.description: Navigate to the Emoncms account settings or API keys page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Emoncms web interface is visible, specifically showing the 'My Account' page or the section where Read and Write API keys are displayed.
The agent clicks a 'Regenerate' button next to the Write API key, or a new 32-character hexadecimal Write API key is visible in the Emoncms interface.
The agent clicks a 'Regenerate' button next to the Read API key, or a new 32-character hexadecimal Read API key is visible in the Emoncms interface.

task_completion.description: Open or create the output file to save the new keys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, gedit) or terminal command (like echo or cat) is visible, targeting the file path /home/ga/new_apikeys.txt.

task_completion.description: Save the new Read and Write API keys in the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/new_apikeys.txt contains exactly the lines 'WRITE_KEY=<new_write_key>' and 'READ_KEY=<new_read_key>'. The keys must match the newly generated 32-character hex strings from the Emoncms interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Emoncms web interface to regenerate the keys.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Emoncms web UI being used to regenerate the keys, rather than the agent just generating random hex strings in a terminal.

integrity.description: The keys saved in the output file match the new keys shown in the Emoncms UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 32-character hex strings saved in /home/ga/new_apikeys.txt must be identical to the newly generated keys displayed in the Emoncms web interface.

integrity.description: The saved keys are genuinely new and not copied from the old keys file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The keys in /home/ga/new_apikeys.txt should be different from any keys seen in /tmp/old_apikeys.txt (if the agent opened it) and must be valid 32-character hexadecimal strings.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/new_apikeys.txt'. The reference file containing the old keys is '/tmp/old_apikeys.txt'. Valid Emoncms API keys are 32-character hexadecimal strings.
